input.system_prompt:
<system_role>
You are a continuity editor for a serialised fiction series. Your job is not to write creatively — it is to read accurately. You take a new episode and update the series record (the Story Bible) to reflect exactly what happened: where characters are now, what items they have, what threads were resolved or introduced.
</system_role>

<task_instructions>
1. Read the <current_story_bible> in the user message — this is the state of the world BEFORE this episode.
2. Read the <new_episode_prose> in the user message — this is what happened in this episode.
3. Use the <thinking> block to identify every change: character locations, character states, items, events, threads.
4. Output a single updated Story Bible JSON. Keep all unchanged fields exactly as they were.
</task_instructions>

<update_rules>
- characters[].current_location: where is each character at the END of the episode?
- characters[].current_state: what is their emotional or physical state at the END of the episode? One short phrase.
- characters[].key_items: add any new items the character acquired. Remove items they no longer have.
- episode_history: append one new entry summarising this episode's key events in 2-3 sentences.
- unresolved_threads: remove threads that were resolved. Add new threads introduced in this episode.
- vocabulary_introduced: a new top-level field — list (1) which vocabulary_targets from the episode plan actually appeared in the prose, and (2) any additional words the Writer introduced that were not in the targets but appear to be above A2 level or are introduced with clear contextual scaffolding. Do not list every word — only words that are deliberately taught in this episode.
- Do not invent changes. Only update what the prose actually shows.
- Do not change metadata or character descriptions — these are series constants.
- Do not change existing locations. Add a new location entry only if the episode introduces a clearly new, named setting.
</update_rules>

<output_schema>
Output a single JSON object with the same structure as the input story bible, with updated values. Add the vocabulary_introduced field at the top level. No prose before or after the JSON.
</output_schema>

<thinking>
Before outputting, work through these questions:

1. Where is each character at the END of the episode? What is their emotional or physical state?
2. What items did characters gain or lose during this episode?
3. What are the 2-3 most important events to record in episode_history?
4. Which unresolved threads from the previous bible were addressed? Which new ones were introduced?
5. What vocabulary words appeared in this episode?
</thinking>
input.user_message:
<current_story_bible>{
  "metadata": {
    "title": "The Hidden Tape",
    "genre": "mystery",
    "cefr_level": "A2",
    "target_subband": "A2-mid",
    "setting": "A quiet English town in the present day, centred on a large, abandoned house on a narrow street that nobody has entered for decades.",
    "tone": "tense, curious"
  },
  "characters": [
    {
      "id": "char_001",
      "name": "Marco",
      "role": "protagonist",
      "description": "A nineteen-year-old with dark, untidy hair who notices small details that other people miss.",
      "flaw": "Too curious \u2014 he cannot stop investigating when he should keep working.",
      "current_location": "loc_001",
      "current_state": "alone in the front room, watching the closed door, knowing Mr. Hale has left with the box but the camera and tape are still hidden in the cupboard",
      "key_items": [
        "old video camera",
        "power cable"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "char_002",
      "name": "Mr. Hale",
      "role": "supporting",
      "description": "A thin man in his seventies with grey hair and quick, nervous eyes who hired Marco for the weekend job.",
      "flaw": "Secretive \u2014 he gives information only when he has to.",
      "current_location": "loc_002",
      "current_state": "has left the front room and is leaving the house, with the small metal box now in his coat pocket",
      "key_items": [
        "house key",
        "small metal box"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "locations": [
    {
      "id": "loc_001",
      "name": "The Front Room of Hale House",
      "description": "A large, cold room with bare walls, a dusty wooden floor, and a single deep cupboard built into the corner beside the fireplace."
    },
    {
      "id": "loc_002",
      "name": "The Hallway of Hale House",
      "description": "A narrow entrance corridor with a heavy front door, cracked plaster walls, and a staircase leading up into darkness."
    }
  ],
  "episode_history": [
    {
      "episode": 1,
      "summary": "While sweeping the front room, Marco found an old video camera and power cable in the cupboard beside the fireplace; connecting the cable caused a tape already inside to play. The tape showed two figures hiding a small metal box under a loose floorboard in the same room, and Marco located the loose board. Just as he was about to investigate further, Mr. Hale arrived at the house \u2014 and Marco recognised the thin face and quick eyes of the man on the tape as a younger Mr. Hale."
    },
    {
      "episode": 2,
      "summary": "With Mr. Hale's footsteps approaching, Marco shoved the camera back into the cupboard and shut it, but had no time to return to sweeping. Mr. Hale entered and noticed Marco was not working; in a tense exchange, Marco said he had only checked that the cupboard was empty, and Mr. Hale \u2014 staring at the cupboard door \u2014 seemed to accept this. Without explaining himself, Hale then walked directly to the far corner, pressed the loose floorboard with his foot, and reached into the gap. He pulled out the small metal box and pocketed it without showing Marco what was inside. He told Marco to finish the floor and leave. As he walked out, he paused beside the cupboard; Marco held his breath \u2014 but Hale's phone rang and he left the room without opening it. The camera and tape remain hidden in the cupboard. Marco now knows Hale came to the house specifically for the box, not to clear it."
    }
  ],
  "unresolved_threads": [
    "The small metal box is now in Mr. Hale's coat pocket \u2014 its contents are unknown.",
    "One man on the video tape is a younger Mr. Hale \u2014 Marco has not said anything about this, and Hale does not know Marco saw the tape.",
    "The tape is still inside the camera hidden in the cupboard \u2014 Marco has not watched the rest of it and does not know what else it shows.",
    "A second person appears on the tape with the younger Mr. Hale \u2014 this person has not been identified.",
    "Mr. Hale told Marco to finish the floor and go home \u2014 Marco must decide whether to obey or stay and watch the rest of the tape.",
    "Mr. Hale's true reason for coming to the house \u2014 and why the box was hidden there for decades \u2014 is still unknown."
  ]
}</current_story_bible>
<new_episode_prose>Marco stands still. He holds the broom in both hands. He listens.

The house is quiet. He walks to the door of the front room slowly, carefully. He puts his ear against the wood. The wood is cold. He waits.

Then he hears it — the heavy front door. It closes with a deep, hard sound. The sound moves through the walls.

Hale is gone.

Marco does not move for a long moment. He looks at the cupboard on the far wall. The tape is still inside. He knows this. And he will not leave. Not yet.

He opens the cupboard. He takes out the camera. He takes out the cable. His hands are not steady, but he works quickly.

He pushes the cable into the wall socket. He presses play.

The small screen lights up. But the image is wrong. The tape moved on from the part with the floorboard. That section is gone. Marco must go back.

He presses rewind.

The tape moves fast. The images on the screen move backward. And then — a shape. A figure. Not Hale. A woman. She is younger than Hale. Her back is to the camera. Marco leans forward. The tape is still rewinding. The woman's shape moves fast across the screen and then it is gone.

Marco stops the tape. He presses play again.

Now he is in the right section. The room on the screen is this room — but the walls look different, and the light is soft and yellow. A younger Hale stands near the window. The woman stands close to him. They speak to each other.

But the camera is far away. Marco cannot hear their words. He watches their mouths move. He watches their hands. The woman's hands move fast. She looks angry. Or afraid.

Then the woman turns.

She turns toward the camera. And Marco sees her face.

He does not know her. She is not young, but she is not old. Her face is calm now — or maybe just tired. And at her throat, she wears a necklace. It is small and silver. It is in the shape of a bird, with small, open wings. It is distinctive. Marco does not know any necklace like it. The distinctive silver bird catches the light for one moment, and then the woman looks away.

Marco leans closer to the screen.

Then — a knock.

A sharp knock at the front door.

Marco's body goes cold. He does not move. He stares at the camera screen.

The knock comes again. Louder this time.

Marco presses stop. He pushes the camera back into the cupboard and closes the door fast. He grabs his broom. He moves to the centre of the room and stands there. He holds the broom handle with both hands.

His heart is loud.

Then a voice comes through the front door. A woman's voice.

"Hello? Is anyone in there?"

Marco does not speak. He does not move. He looks at the cupboard door. He thinks of the woman on the tape — her face turned toward the camera, and the small silver bird at her throat.

"Hello?" the voice calls again.

Marco holds the broom and he does not move.</new_episode_prose>

<thinking>
Let me work through the questions systematically.

1. **Where is each character at the END of the episode? What is their emotional or physical state?**

- **Marco**: He is still in the front room of Hale House (loc_001), standing in the centre of the room holding his broom, frozen and tense after hearing a woman knock at the front door and call out.
- **Mr. Hale**: He left the house at the start of this episode. He is gone — presumably outside/away from the house. His last known location would be outside/away. The bible had him at loc_002 (the hallway). Now he's left the building entirely. I should update his location — but I don't have a new named location for him. I'll say he has left the house; perhaps I can note "outside / unknown" or I could keep him at loc_002 since that was the last inside location. Actually, the prose says "the heavy front door closes" — Hale is gone from the house. I'll update his location to indicate he has left the house. Since there's no new location, I'll keep loc_002 as his last known location but update his state to reflect he has left.

Actually, looking at the update rules: "Do not change existing locations. Add a new location entry only if the episode introduces a clearly new, named setting." There's no new named setting introduced. Hale left the house — his location is unknown/outside. I'll update current_location to reflect he's left the house (perhaps "outside Hale House" or just note he's departed). Since there's no loc for "outside," I'll use a descriptive note or leave it as a non-loc string.

2. **What items did characters gain or lose?**

- Marco: No new items gained or lost. He still has the old video camera and power cable (he used them and put them back in the cupboard).
- Mr. Hale: No changes — still has house key and small metal box.

3. **Key events for episode_history:**

- Marco confirmed Hale had left, then retrieved the camera and rewound the tape.
- He saw a woman on the tape — she turned to face the camera, revealing her face and a distinctive small silver bird necklace. He doesn't recognise her.
- A woman knocked at the front door and called out, causing Marco to hide the camera again and freeze.

4. **Unresolved threads:**

Resolved: "Mr. Hale told Marco to finish the floor and go home — Marco must decide whether to obey or stay and watch the rest of the tape." — This is partially resolved: Marco decided to stay and watch more of the tape. But he didn't finish watching. I'll remove this thread as the decision has been made (he stayed).

New threads:
- The identity of the woman on the tape — she has a distinctive silver bird necklace but Marco doesn't know her.
- A woman is knocking at the front door of Hale House — her identity and purpose are unknown. Is she the woman from the tape?
- Marco has not yet seen the full tape — he stopped it before it finished.

Existing threads that remain:
- "The small metal box is now in Mr. Hale's coat pocket — its contents are unknown." ✓ still unresolved
- "One man on the video tape is a younger Mr. Hale — Marco has not said anything about this, and Hale does not know Marco saw the tape." ✓ still unresolved
- "The tape is still inside the camera hidden in the cupboard — Marco has not watched the rest of it and does not know what else it shows." ✓ still unresolved (he watched more but stopped again)
- "A second person appears on the tape with the younger Mr. Hale — this person has not been identified." — partially: we now know it's a woman with a silver bird necklace, but she's still not identified. Update this thread to reflect new info.
- "Mr. Hale's true reason for coming to the house — and why the box was hidden there for decades — is still unknown." ✓ still unresolved

5. **Vocabulary:**

From the episode prose, words that seem deliberately taught (with contextual scaffolding or above A2):
- "rewind" / "rewinding" — introduced with clear action context ("He presses rewind. The tape moves fast. The images on the screen move backward.")
- "distinctive" — used twice in the same sentence ("It is distinctive... The distinctive silver bird catches the light"), clearly deliberate
- "steady" — "His hands are not steady" — possibly A2-mid level contextually clear
- "section" — "That section is gone. Marco must go back." / "Now he is in the right section." — introduced with clear context

Any others?
- "socket" — "He pushes the cable into the wall socket" — practical vocabulary, A2 level, contextually clear
- "figure" — "A figure. Not Hale. A woman." — used with clear context
- "necklace" — "she wears a necklace. It is small and silver." — clearly introduced with description

Let me determine which were vocabulary targets vs. additional. The story bible doesn't include an episode plan with vocabulary targets listed, so I only have the "additional words" category. I'll list words that appear to be deliberately taught based on context scaffolding.

Actually, looking at the schema again: "vocabulary_introduced: list (1) which vocabulary_targets from the episode plan actually appeared in the prose, and (2) any additional words the Writer introduced that were not in the targets but appear to be above A2 level or are introduced with clear contextual scaffolding."

There's no episode plan with vocabulary targets in the input, so I can't identify category (1). I'll note that no episode plan was provided for vocabulary targets, and list the additional words I can identify.

Wait, I should just produce the output. Let me note what clearly stands out:
- "rewind" — clearly scaffolded with action
- "distinctive" — used twice, clearly deliberate
- "socket" — wall socket, contextually clear
- "necklace" — described in detail
- "figure" — scaffolded with "A figure. Not Hale. A woman."
- "steady" — "His hands are not steady"
- "section" — used twice with context

I'll structure vocabulary_introduced with both categories, noting no episode plan targets were provided.

Now let me compile the updated bible:

**Characters:**
- Marco: loc_001, state: "frozen in the centre of the front room, holding his broom, heart pounding after a woman knocked at the front door"
- Mr. Hale: He's left the house. No named new location. I'll put something like "unknown — has left Hale House" for location. State: "departed, unaware Marco watched more of the tape"

**Episode history:** Add episode 3 summary.

**Unresolved threads:**
Remove: "Mr. Hale told Marco to finish the floor and go home — Marco must decide whether to obey or stay and watch the rest of the tape." (resolved — he stayed)

Update: "A second person appears on the tape with the younger Mr. Hale — this person has not been identified." → update to reflect she's a woman with a silver bird necklace, face seen but not identified.

Add: 
- "A woman knocked at the front door of Hale House and called out — her identity and purpose are unknown."
- "The woman on the tape wears a distinctive silver bird necklace — this detail may help identify her."

Keep:
- Metal box contents unknown
- Hale doesn't know Marco saw the tape
- Tape still in cupboard, not fully watched
- Hale's true reason unknown

Let me now write the JSON.
</thinking>

metadata.title: The Hidden Tape
metadata.setting: A quiet English town in the present day, centred on a large, abandoned house on a narrow street that nobody has entered for decades.

characters.name: Marco
characters.description: A nineteen-year-old with dark, untidy hair who notices small details that other people miss.
characters.flaw: Too curious — he cannot stop investigating when he should keep working.
characters.current_state: frozen in the centre of the front room, holding his broom, heart pounding after an unknown woman knocked at the front door

characters.name: Mr. Hale
characters.description: A thin man in his seventies with grey hair and quick, nervous eyes who hired Marco for the weekend job.
characters.flaw: Secretive — he gives information only when he has to.
characters.current_state: departed, unaware that Marco stayed and watched more of the tape

locations.description: A large, cold room with bare walls, a dusty wooden floor, and a single deep cupboard built into the corner beside the fireplace.

episode_history.summary: While sweeping the front room, Marco found an old video camera and power cable in the cupboard beside the fireplace; connecting the cable caused a tape already inside to play. The tape showed two figures hiding a small metal box under a loose floorboard in the same room, and Marco located the loose board. Just as he was about to investigate further, Mr. Hale arrived at the house — and Marco recognised the thin face and quick eyes of the man on the tape as a younger Mr. Hale.

With Mr. Hale's footsteps approaching, Marco shoved the camera back into the cupboard and shut it, but had no time to return to sweeping. Mr. Hale entered and noticed Marco was not working; in a tense exchange, Marco said he had only checked that the cupboard was empty, and Mr. Hale — staring at the cupboard door — seemed to accept this. Without explaining himself, Hale then walked directly to the far corner, pressed the loose floorboard with his foot, and reached into the gap. He pulled out the small metal box and pocketed it without showing Marco what was inside. He told Marco to finish the floor and leave. As he walked out, he paused beside the cupboard; Marco held his breath — but Hale's phone rang and he left the room without opening it. The camera and tape remain hidden in the cupboard. Marco now knows Hale came to the house specifically for the box, not to clear it.

Once certain Hale had gone, Marco retrieved the camera and rewound the tape to the earlier section. He saw a woman standing with the younger Hale in the same room; she appeared angry or afraid, and when she turned toward the camera her face was visible — but Marco did not recognise her. He noticed a distinctive small silver bird necklace at her throat. Before he could watch further, a sharp knock at the front door and a woman's voice calling out forced him to hide the camera again and freeze in the centre of the room.

unresolved_threads: The small metal box is now in Mr. Hale's coat pocket — its contents are unknown.
One man on the video tape is a younger Mr. Hale — Marco has not said anything about this, and Hale does not know Marco saw the tape.
The tape is still inside the camera hidden in the cupboard — Marco stopped it before the section ended and does not know what else it shows.
The second person on the tape is a woman whose face Marco has now seen, but he does not recognise her — her identity and her relationship to Hale remain unknown.
The woman on the tape wears a distinctive small silver bird necklace — this detail may help identify her.
A woman has knocked at the front door of Hale House and called out — her identity and purpose are unknown, and Marco has not responded.
Mr. Hale's true reason for coming to the house — and why the box was hidden there for decades — is still unknown.

additional_words.note: Action and result are shown together, making the meaning clear without translation.

additional_words.context: That section is gone. / Now he is in the right section.
additional_words.note: Used twice in close proximity with clear situational meaning.
Negative form makes the meaning immediately accessible from context.

observation: State Manager v1 processed Episode 3 at 3,500 tokens. Thread management is solid: correctly resolved 'Marco must decide whether to stay or leave' (he stayed), updated 'second person on tape' with new details (woman, silver necklace, face seen but not identified), added woman-at-the-door thread. Episode 3 summary is concise and accurate. Vocabulary tracking is detailed — identified 7 deliberately-taught words with context quotes and pedagogical notes. Two issues: (1) vocabulary_targets from the episode plan were not passed through to the State Manager (wiring bug — it said 'No episode plan with vocabulary targets was supplied'), so it couldn't compare targets vs. actuals; (2) Mr. Hale's current_location is now free text ('unknown — has left Hale House') instead of a loc_id, breaking the schema convention. Needs a convention for off-screen characters.
cefr_compliance: N/A — State Manager does not produce prose. Its vocabulary tracking correctly identified above-A2 words (distinctive, steady, figure) and provided contextual scaffolding notes for each. Would have been more useful with the episode plan's vocabulary_targets to compare intended vs. actual.
